summary: HNSW remains functional-but-not-leading on the current remote x86 authority lane: recall and production contract gates are closed, fairness inputs are aligned (including datatype), and the latest no-sqrt BF16 kernel refresh narrows native lead to about 1.24x on the fair lane.
action: Archive HNSW as functional-but-not-leading, allow production-contract usage to rely on the closed FFI/persistence evidence, and keep HNSW out of performance-leadership claims until a new fair-lane authority artifact closes the remaining ~1.24x throughput gap.
